skill.name: unstructured-medical-text-miner
skill.description: Extract structured insights from unstructured clinical text using NLP and pattern matching.
skill.summary: Transform raw medical notes from MIMIC-IV into structured entities, relations, and clinical logic.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 74 static analysis findings are false positives. The external_commands patterns appear in markdown documentation showing shell commands in code blocks, not in executable injection points. The filesystem patterns are legitimate read/write operations for the skill's core purpose. The weak cryptography and path traversal detections are triggered by documentation text describing security measures, not actual vulnerable code. The legacy audit scored this skill 85/100 with Production Ready status.

medium_findings.title: External commands in markdown code blocks
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer detected shell backtick execution patterns in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md. These are all inside markdown code blocks showing example shell commands for users to run manually. No actual command injection or dynamic execution exists in the code. The 39 locations are all documentation examples.

medium_findings.title: File write operation for JSON export
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: File write at scripts/main.py:524 is the export_to_json method that writes structured results to an output file. This is a core and expected function of the skill. The output path is user-specified via CLI argument, not hardcoded or attacker-controlled.

medium_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The 10 locations flagged for weak cryptography are all within JSON output examples in SKILL.md markdown and unrelated string literals in scripts/main.py. No cryptographic operations exist in the codebase.

medium_findings.title: Heuristic dangerous combination alert
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged a combination of code execution, network, and credential access patterns. These patterns are spread across disconnected documentation examples and legitimate library imports. No actual coordinated malicious behavior exists. The skill makes zero network calls and has no credential handling code.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance pattern in data filtering
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Patterns flagged as system reconnaissance are standard pandas DataFrame filtering operations (scripts/main.py:171-172 uses df['subject_id'] == subject_id). These are patient record lookups, not system enumeration.

low_findings.title: Path traversal detection in security checklist
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md:287 and :290 are inside a Security Checklist section warning against path traversal (../). The text describes what to avoid, not actual path traversal code.

low_findings.title: SQLite database reference in CLI example
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: SKILL.md:257 mentions --db-path mimic_iv.db in a CLI usage example code block. This is documentation showing how to use the tool, not an actual database connection in executable code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in references section
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: URLs in SKILL.md:7, 263, 264 are reference links to the MIMIC-IV dataset and scispacy library documentation. No network requests are made by the Python code. These are static informational URLs in markdown.

low_findings.title: Config model name lookup
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: scripts/main.py:110 reads ner_model from config dictionary. This selects which NLP model to use for entity extraction. It does not access environment variables or secrets.

content.user_title: Extract Medical Insights from Clinical Notes
content.value_statement: Unstructured clinical text in MIMIC-IV contains diagnostic logic and treatment reasoning hidden in discharge summaries and progress reports. This skill extracts entities, relationships, timelines, and clinical logic from raw medical text into structured formats.
actual_capabilities: Extract diseases, symptoms, medications, procedures, and anatomy terms from clinical text using regex patterns and spaCy NLP
Detect negated clinical findings by checking for negation words like no, not, denies, and without in surrounding text context
Parse diagnostic reasoning chains from chief complaint through differential diagnosis to final diagnosis and treatment plan
Extract treatment decision logic including medication-disease relationships and symptom-disease diagnostic links
Build temporal event sequences to track disease progression from admission through discharge
Export structured extraction results in JSON format for downstream analysis and reporting
limitations: Requires MIMIC-IV data access or compatible medical text input in CSV or Parquet format
Regex-based entity extraction may miss uncommon or misspelled medical terms not in the pattern list
Does not support image or PDF text extraction, only plain text input
Relation extraction uses simple pattern matching and may miss complex or indirect clinical relationships

use_cases.title: Retrospective Clinical Study Data Extraction
use_cases.description: Extract structured data from thousands of discharge summaries for population-level analysis of treatment patterns and outcomes.

use_cases.title: Medical Record Entity Indexing
use_cases.description: Transform unstructured clinical notes into searchable entity indexes for fast retrieval of patient conditions, medications, and procedures.

use_cases.title: Clinical NLP Training Data Generation
use_cases.description: Generate labeled entity and relation training data from real clinical text to build custom medical NLP models.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Entity Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt:
Extract all medications, diseases, and symptoms mentioned in this clinical text. List each entity with its type:

[Insert clinical text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick extraction of key medical concepts from a single note

prompt_templates.title: Negation-Aware Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt:
Analyze this medical text and extract all clinical findings. For each finding, indicate whether it is PRESENT or NEGATED. A finding is negated if words like no, not, denies, or without appear before it:

[Insert clinical text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Distinguishing confirmed from ruled-out conditions in clinical notes

prompt_templates.title: Full Diagnostic Logic Parsing
prompt_templates.prompt:
Parse this clinical text and extract the full diagnostic reasoning chain. Include the presenting complaint, differential diagnoses, workup performed, final diagnosis, and treatment plan. Structure the output by each reasoning step:

[Insert clinical text here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding clinical decision-making from admission to discharge

prompt_templates.title: Batch Patient Processing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all clinical notes for patient 10000032. Load the NOTEEVENTS data from noteevents.csv, filter for discharge summaries and radiology reports, extract all entities and relations, build the clinical logic chain, and export the complete insights to patient_insights.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: End-to-end batch processing of a patient record using the full MedicalTextMiner pipeline

output_examples.input: Patient presented with acute chest pain radiating to left arm. ECG showed ST elevation in V1-V4 consistent with anterior STEMI. Started on aspirin 81mg and clopidogrel 75mg.
output_examples.output: Extracted entities: chest pain (symptom), ST elevation (finding), STEMI (disease), aspirin 81mg (medication), clopidogrel 75mg (medication). Clinical logic identified STEMI as the final diagnosis with PCI as the treatment plan. Two medication entities detected with dosage information.

output_examples.input: No evidence of pneumonia. Patient denies dyspnea or fever. Chest X-ray showed clear lung fields.
output_examples.output: Three negated findings detected: pneumonia is ruled out, dyspnea is denied, fever is denied. One positive finding: clear lung fields. Negation cues were identified for all three negated entities based on surrounding text context.

output_examples.input: Patient admitted on 03/15 with worsening shortness of breath. Developed hypotension on day 2. Started on norepinephrine drip. Improved by day 4 and discharged on day 5.
output_examples.output: Temporal events extracted: admission (03/15), worsening SOB (day 1), hypotension onset (day 2), norepinephrine start (day 2), improvement (day 4), discharge (day 5). Clinical course shows progression from respiratory distress to hemodynamic instability to recovery.

best_practices: Verify that input text is de-identified before processing to protect patient privacy and comply with data use agreements
Review negated findings separately from confirmed findings during analysis, as they represent ruled-out conditions
Validate entity extraction quality against a small sample set before running full batch processing on large datasets
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for real-time clinical decision support without human review of all extracted findings
Do not process protected health information without proper data use agreements and institutional review board approval
Do not treat extracted entities as definitive medical diagnoses without verification against the original clinical record

faq.question: What data sources does this skill support?
faq.answer: MIMIC-IV NOTEEVENTS in CSV or Parquet format and direct plain text input through the sample-text option.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally on your machine with no external API calls or data transmission.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without MIMIC-IV data?
faq.answer: Yes. The sample-text option lets you process any medical text directly without MIMIC-IV data files.

faq.question: What NLP models does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses spaCy with configurable models and regex-based pattern matching. The default model is en_core_sci_lg.

faq.question: How does negation detection work?
faq.answer: It checks for negation words like no, not, denies, without, and negative for in the 50 characters before each entity.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: JSON format with options for FHIR-compatible clinical documents and knowledge graph triple formats.
